report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 64-year-old female with a history of adenocarcinoma of the lung, presenting for follow-up evaluation.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest was performed following the intravenous administration of 100 mL of IOPAMIDOL 76% IV SOLN. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images were reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 4/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Right upper lobe spiculated mass measuring 25 x 18 mm (image 73/series 2), increased from 20 x 15 mm previously. Additional right middle lobe nodule measuring 8 mm (image 114/series 2), stable. Multiple bilateral subcentimeter nodules, consistent with metastatic disease. Mild centrilobular emphysema. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No filling defects in the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Main pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications. Mild coronary artery calcification noted. Esophagus is nondistended, limiting assessment. Small hiatal hernia present.

Lymph Nodes:
Multiple enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes, the largest in the right paratracheal region measuring 15 x 10 mm (image 56/series 2). Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 12 x 10 mm (image 69/series 2).

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine with no evidence of acute fracture or suspicious osseous lesions. No significant soft tissue abnormalities.

Upper Abdomen:
Adrenal glands are normal in appearance bilaterally. Visualized portions of the liver, spleen, pancreas, kidneys are unremarkable. Few hepatic cysts seen.
impression:
1. Right upper lobe spiculated mass enlargement concerning for progression of adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend PET-CT for further evaluation.
   
2. Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic disease.
   Recommend continued imaging follow-up with chest CT in three months.
   
3. Enlarged mediastinal and right hilar lymph nodes suggestive of metastatic involvement.
   Recommend tissue sampling for histopathological confirmation.
   
4. Mild centrilobular emphysema.
   
5. Incidental small hiatal hernia.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 65-year-old male with history of adenocarcinoma presenting for staging and follow-up assessment.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iohexol 350 mg/mL. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 5/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Numerous bilateral pulmonary nodules, some increasing in size. Largest nodule in the right upper lobe measures 16 x 14 mm, previously 10 x 8 mm (image 114/series 2). Another nodule in the left lower lobe measures 12 x 10 mm, previously 8 x 7 mm (image 130/series 2). Multiple smaller nodules ranging from 3-6 mm scattered throughout both lungs, some demonstrating interval growth. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Main pulmonary arteries are patent with no evidence of pulmonary embolism. Normal caliber without filling defects.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size is normal. No pericardial effusion. Atherosclerotic calcifications noted in the coronary arteries. Thoracic aorta normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic changes. Small hiatal hernia present. Esophagus nondistended but appears normal.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes measuring up to 15 mm in short axis at the right paratracheal region (image 145/series 2), previously measured at 12 mm. Enlarged subcarinal lymph node measuring 18 x 15 mm (image 160/series 2), previously measured at 14 x 11 mm. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine without evidence of acute fracture or suspicious osseous lesion.

Upper Abdomen:
Adrenal glands are normal bilaterally. Visualized portions of liver, spleen, pancreas, and kidneys are unremarkable. See separately dictated abdominal CT for further details.
impression:
1. Progression of multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend follow-up PET-CT for further characterization.
   
2. Enlarging mediastinal lymph nodes suspicious for metastatic involvement.
   Suggest biopsy of the largest node to confirm pathology.
   
3. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism.

4. Incidental finding of small hiatal hernia without complications.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 62-year-old male presenting for evaluation of adenocarcinoma of the lung.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iohexol 350 mg/mL. Axial, coronal, sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 3/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Central airways patent. 
Left lower lobe mass measuring 45 x 30 mm, demonstrating heterogeneous enhancement with central necrosis suspicious for adenocarcinoma.
Moderate left pleural effusion with associated compressive atelectasis.
No pneumothorax.
Multiple bilateral subcentimeter pulmonary nodules, stable compared to prior examination. 
Mild centrilobular emphysema.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No pulmonary embolism. 
Normal caliber main pulmonary artery.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.
Severe coronary artery calcifications.
Atherosclerotic disease of the thoracic aorta without aneurysm.
Small hiatal hernia.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged left hilar lymph node measuring 15 x 10 mm. No other mediastinal or axillary lymphadenopathy noted.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes in thoracic spine. No aggressive osseous lesion. Osteopenia limits detection of small lytic lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Adrenal glands unremarkable.
Few hepatic cysts.

**
impression:
1. Large heterogeneous enhancing mass in the left lower lobe suspicious for adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend PET-CT for further evaluation and tissue sampling for definitive diagnosis.
2. Enlarged left hilar lymph node suggestive of metastatic involvement.
   Recommend follow-up imaging to assess interval changes.
3. Moderate left pleural effusion with associated compressive atelectasis.
   Recommend thoracentesis if symptomatic.

report:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INFORMATION: 65-year-old male presenting for evaluation of adenocarcinoma.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 7/3/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Right upper lobe spiculated mass measuring 4.5 x 3.8 cm on image 112/series 3, consistent with primary adenocarcinoma. No new suspicious pulmonary nodules. Mild centrilobular emphysema. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Normal caliber pulmonary arteries. No filling defects to suggest pulmonary embolism.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Normal heart size. No pericardial effusion. Moderate coronary artery calcifications. Mildly dilated ascending aorta measuring up to 42 mm on image 90/series 3. Small hiatal hernia.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged right hilar node measuring 12 x 10 mm on image 137/series 3. Enlarged subcarinal lymph node measuring 16 x 14 mm on image 145/series 3.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Unremarkable visualized portions.
impression:
1. Spiculated right upper lobe mass consistent with primary adenocarcinoma.
Recommend PET-CT for further characterization and staging.
2. Enlarged right hilar and subcarinal lymph nodes, likely metastatic.
Recommend endobronchial ultrasound-guided biopsy for tissue diagnosis.
3. Mild centrilobular emphysema.
4. Moderate coronary artery calcifications and mild aortic dilation.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 72-year-old male with biopsy-proven adenocarcinoma of the lung.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76%. Axial, coronal, sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 8/12/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura: 
Multiple pulmonary nodules, largest at right upper lobe measuring 28 x 22 mm (image 145/series 2), consistent with known adenocarcinoma. New right lower lobe nodule measuring 12 x 9 mm (image 210/series 2). Stable left lower lobe subpleural nodule, 6 mm. Mild centrilobular emphysema. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No pulmonary embolism. Normal caliber main, lobar, segmental pulmonary arteries.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Normal heart size. Severe coronary artery calcification. No pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta with mild atherosclerotic changes. Small hiatal hernia. Right internal jugular port terminates in the superior vena cava.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 15 x 10 mm (image 160/series 2), consistent with metastasis. No axillary adenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesion.

Upper Abdomen:
Unremarkable adrenal glands. Few hepatic cysts.

**
impression:
1. Large right upper lobe lung mass consistent with adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend follow-up PET-CT for further characterization.
   
2. New right lower lobe pulmonary nodule suspicious for metastasis.
   Recommend follow-up CT in 3 months.
   
3. Enlarged right hilar lymph node consistent with metastatic disease.
   Recommend thoracic surgery consultation for potential biopsy.

4. Severe coronary artery calcification.
   Consider cardiology consultation for ischemic evaluation if clinically indicated.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 58-year-old male with recently diagnosed adenocarcinoma.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 7/10/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Large spiculated mass in the right upper lobe measuring 34 x 28 mm, showing irregular margins and moderate enhancement, consistent with primary adenocarcinoma. Multiple small nodules in the right lower lobe, the largest measuring 6 mm on image 98/series 4, likely metastatic, stable compared to prior. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Main pulmonary arteries are patent without filling defects. No evidence of pulmonary embolism.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Mediastinal widening due to bulky lymphadenopathy. Enlarged subcarinal lymph node measuring 18 x 14 mm. Normal heart size with no pericardial effusion. Aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 15 x 12 mm. No axillary lymphadenopathy detected. 

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
No suspicious osseous lesions. Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine.

Upper Abdomen:
Visualized portions of the liver, spleen, pancreas, and kidneys are unremarkable. Adrenal glands appear normal.
impression:
1. Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe consistent with primary adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend PET-CT for further evaluation.
2. Enlarged subcarinal and right hilar lymph nodes suggestive of metastatic involvement.
   Recommend tissue sampling via endobronchial ultrasound-guided biopsy.
3. Multiple stable small nodules in the right lower lobe likely metastatic.
4. No evidence of pleural effusion or new metastatic disease in visualized upper abdomen.

FOLLOW-UP NOTICE: A finding is present that may require medical care within 3 months. This report has been flagged to inform the patient to contact their ordering provider to discuss the finding.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 65-year-old male presenting for evaluation of adenocarcinoma, status post chemotherapy.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 6/12/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules, predominantly in the right upper lobe, with the largest measuring 15 mm in diameter (image 114/series 2). Interval increase from 10 mm previously. New 8 mm nodule in the left lower lobe. Mild centrilobular emphysema predominantly in the upper lobes. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. Persistent fibrotic changes in the left lower lobe.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No filling defects identified within the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Normal caliber of main pulmonary artery.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Mild cardiomegaly with otherwise normal heart size and morphology. No pericardial effusion. Aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications. Small hiatal hernia noted.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 14 x 12 mm (previously 13 x 11 mm). No other mediastinal or axillary lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No aggressive osseous lesions. Scattered sclerotic foci within the ribs consistent with treated metastatic disease.

Upper Abdomen:
Visualized liver demonstrates a few small hepatic cysts. Adrenal glands are normal in size and appearance.
impression:
1. Progressive increase in size of multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma.
   Recommend follow-up PET-CT to assess metabolic activity of these nodules.
   
2. Enlarged right hilar lymph node, likely metastatic.
   Recommend tissue biopsy if clinically indicated.

3. Mild centrilobular emphysema.
   
4. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism.

5. Scattered sclerotic foci within ribs consistent with treated metastatic disease.

report:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INFORMATION: 54-year-old male presenting for evaluation of adenocarcinoma. History of smoking and previous left upper lobe resection.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 7/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Post-surgical changes in the left upper lobe consistent with prior resection. New spiculated nodule in the right lower lobe measuring 18 x 15 mm (image 134/series 5), highly suspicious for malignancy. Mildly increased groundglass opacity surrounding the nodule. Patchy consolidation in the right middle lobe, likely post-inflammatory or infectious. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No filling defects in the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Normal caliber of the main pulmonary artery.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Normal heart size. No pericardial effusion. The aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcification. No mediastinal mass. Small hiatal hernia.

Lymph Nodes:
Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 12 x 10 mm, increased from 9 x 7 mm on prior exam, suspicious for metastatic involvement. No other enlarged mediastinal or axillary lymph nodes.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions or fractures.

Upper Abdomen:
Visualized portions are unremarkable. Normal adrenal glands bilaterally.

**
impression:
1. Spiculated nodule in the right lower lobe suspicious for malignancy.
   Recommend follow-up PET-CT for further evaluation.
2. Enlarged right hilar lymph node concerning for metastatic spread.
   Recommend tissue sampling via EBUS-TBNA.
3. Post-surgical changes in the left upper lobe consistent with prior resection.
4. Patchy consolidation in the right middle lobe likely post-inflammatory or infectious; clinical correlation advised.
5. Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the aorta.

impression:

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION:  
65-year-old male presenting for evaluation of known adenocarcinoma of the lung with recent weight loss and persistent cough.

PROCEDURE:  
Contrast-enhanced CT chest was performed following the intravenous administration of 80 mL of Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images were reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON:  
Chest CT dated 6/12/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:  
Large spiculated mass in the right upper lobe measuring 45 x 38 mm, increased from 40 x 34 mm on prior study, consistent with primary adenocarcinoma. Adjacent to the mass, there is associated collapse-consolidation and moderate volume loss of the right upper lobe. Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 8 mm in the right lower lobe, previously 7 mm. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:  
No filling defects in the main, lobar, segmental or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Normal caliber of main pulmonary artery.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:  
Heart size normal. Small pericardial effusion. Extensive atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta and coronary arteries. No aneurysm or dissection. Small hiatal hernia noted. Right paratracheal soft tissue density measuring 12 x 10 mm, stable in size.

Lymph Nodes:  
Multiple enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes noted; largest in the subcarinal region measuring 15 x 11 mm (previously 14 x 10 mm). Enlarged right hilar lymph node measuring 18 x 13 mm (previously 16 x 12 mm). No axillary lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:  
Diffuse osteopenia with mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions or fractures.

Upper Abdomen:  
Unremarkable except for a few small hepatic cysts and normal adrenal glands.

**
impression:
1. Progressive right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung adenocarcinoma with associated collapse-consolidation.
   Recommend PET-CT for further characterization and staging.
   
2. Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 8 mm in the right lower lobe.
   Recommend follow-up chest CT in 3 months to monitor nodule progression.
   
3. Enlarged mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes suggestive of metastatic involvement.
   Recommend tissue sampling via EBUS-TBNA for histological confirmation.

4. Small pericardial effusion likely secondary to primary malignancy.
   Clinical correlation advised.

5. Extensive coronary artery atherosclerosis and small hiatal hernia incidentally noted.

Pulmonary Vasculature:  
Main pulmonary arteries are patent without filling defects. Normal caliber of main and lobar pulmonary arteries.

Lymph Nodes:

Skeleton and Chest Wall:  
No suspicious osseous lesions or fractures. Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine.

Upper Abdomen:  
Unremarkable visualized portions of the liver, spleen, pancreas, kidneys, and adrenal glands.

impression:

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 50-year-old male presenting for evaluation of Large Hodgkin Lymphoma.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV SOLN. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Prior chest CT dated 6/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Multiple enlarged mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes causing mass effect on adjacent structures. Right paratracheal lymph node measuring 3.5 x 2.2 cm on image 112/series 2, previously 2.8 x 1.9 cm. Left hilar node measuring 2.6 x 1.4 cm, previously 2.0 x 1.2 cm. Mild patchy consolidation at the right lower lobe, likely secondary to lymphatic obstruction or infection. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No pulmonary embolism identified within the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries. Normal caliber of the main pulmonary artery.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Large anterior mediastinal mass measures approximately 8.4 x 5.6 cm, encasing the ascending aorta but without dissection or significant luminal narrowing. Heart size is normal with no pericardial effusion. Atherosclerotic calcifications in the thoracic aorta without aneurysm or dissection.

Lymph Nodes:
Extensive conglomerate nodal masses within the mediastinum and hila suggestive of lymphoma involvement. Enlarged prevascular nodes measuring up to 4.1 x 3.0 cm on image 90/series 2.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes in the thoracic spine with mild osteopenia but no aggressive osseous lesions identified. No rib fractures or suspicious bone lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Adrenal glands are normal bilaterally. Few hepatic cysts in the visualized portion of the liver.

**
impression:
1. Extensive mediastinal and hilar lymphadenopathy consistent with known Large Hodgkin Lymphoma.
   Recommend follow-up imaging in 3 months to assess treatment response.
   
2. Large anterior mediastinal mass encasing the ascending aorta without evidence of dissection.
   Recommend correlation with PET-CT for further characterization.
   
3. Mild patchy consolidation in the right lower lobe likely secondary to lymphatic obstruction or infection.
   Recommend clinical correlation and follow-up imaging as clinically indicated.
   
4. Incidental hepatic cysts.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Large enhancing anterior mediastinal mass measuring 8 x 6 cm with areas of necrosis and heterogeneous enhancement, consistent with lymphoma. No pericardial effusion. Normal heart size and morphology. Mild atherosclerotic calcification in the thoracic aorta. Small hiatal hernia noted.

impression:

impression:

Normal caliber of the main pulmonary artery and its branches. No filling defects identified to suggest pulmonary embolism.

MEDIASTINUM, HEART, AND GREAT VESSELS:
Mild right ventricular enlargement. No pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber. Moderate calcifications of the coronary arteries. Small hiatal hernia noted.

LYMPH NODES:

SKELETON AND CHEST WALL:
Severe degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No aggressive osseous lesions. 

UPPER ABDOMEN:

impression:

report:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INFORMATION: 70-year-old male presenting for evaluation of pulmonary fibrosis.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV SOLN. Axial, coronal, sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 5/10/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura: 
Diffuse bilateral reticular opacities with honeycombing predominantly in the subpleural regions of both lower lobes, consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern. Patchy groundglass opacities scattered throughout both lungs, more prominent at the lung bases.
No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature: 
Main pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber without filling defects. No evidence of pulmonary embolism.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels: 
Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber, showing mild atherosclerotic calcifications. No aneurysm or dissection.
Small hiatal hernia noted.

Lymph Nodes: 
No mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall: 
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen: 
Visualized portions are unremarkable. Adrenal glands appear normal.

**
impression:
1. Findings consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern of pulmonary fibrosis.
   Recommend follow-up CT in 6 months to monitor disease progression.
2. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism.
3. Mild atherosclerotic disease of the thoracic aorta.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 65-year-old male presenting for evaluation of known pulmonary fibrosis with progressive dyspnea.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% IV SOLN. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 6/15/2022.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura: 
Extensive bilateral reticulation and honeycombing predominantly in the lower lung zones, consistent with advanced pulmonary fibrosis. Traction bronchiectasis is present throughout both lungs, more pronounced in the lower lobes. No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses identified. Mild dependent subsegmental atelectasis in the lower lobes. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature: 
No filling defects in the main, lobar or segmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Main pulmonary artery measures within normal limits.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels: 
Normal heart size with no pericardial effusion. Moderate calcification of the coronary arteries. Atherosclerotic calcifications of the thoracic aorta without aneurysm. No mediastinal masses or significant lymphadenopathy. Small hiatal hernia present.

Lymph Nodes: 
No enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes by CT size criteria.

Skeleton and Chest Wall: 
Degenerative changes noted in the thoracic spine with mild osteopenia. No acute fractures or aggressive osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen: 
Few hepatic cysts noted. Adrenals are unremarkable.

**
impression:
1. Advanced pulmonary fibrosis with extensive bilateral reticulation and honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis more pronounced in lower lobes.
   Recommend follow-up chest CT in 12 months to monitor progression.
2. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism.
3. Moderate coronary artery calcifications.
4. Small hiatal hernia and hepatic cysts which are incidental findings.

report:

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iopamidol 76% contrast. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

Extensive bilateral reticulation and honeycombing predominantly in the subpleural and basal regions, consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern. Traction bronchiectasis noted throughout the lung bases. No new nodules or focal consolidation. Mild centrilobular emphysema predominantly in the upper lobes. Small right pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Main pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber. No filling defects to suggest pulmonary embolism. No evidence of right heart strain.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size normal. Moderate coronary artery calcifications. Thoracic aorta is normal in caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications. No pericardial effusion. Small hiatal hernia noted.

Lymph Nodes:
No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine without acute fracture or suspicious osseous lesion. Stable osteopenia.

Upper Abdomen:
Few hepatic cysts. Adrenal glands are unremarkable.

**
impression:
1. Findings consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia pattern of pulmonary fibrosis.
   Recommend follow-up CT in one year to evaluate disease progression.
2. Mild centrilobular emphysema, likely related to smoking history.
3. Small right pleural effusion, likely reactive.

report:

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Moderate coronary artery calcification. Heart size normal with no pericardial effusion. Aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic changes. Small hiatal hernia noted. Esophagus and thyroid grossly unremarkable.

No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy identified.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes in the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Few hepatic cysts noted. Adrenals appear normal.

impression:
1. Findings consistent with advanced idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) showing a usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern.
   Recommend follow-up CT in 6 months for disease progression assessment.
2. Moderate coronary artery calcifications.
3. Few hepatic cysts, likely benign.

report:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INFORMATION: 72-year-old male with a history of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, presenting for evaluation of disease progression.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL of Iopamidol 76%. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT 4/15/2023.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Diffuse subpleural reticulations, traction bronchiectasis, and honeycombing predominantly in the bilateral lower lobes and peripheral regions, consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP). Mildly increased extent of fibrosis compared to prior study. Minimal groundglass opacities in the upper lobes, stable from previous imaging. Small bilateral pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Main pulmonary artery measures 32 mm in diameter, within normal limits. No filling defects identified in the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion. Mild coronary artery calcifications. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber with moderate atherosclerotic changes. No evidence of dissection or aneurysm. Right infrahilar lymph node measuring 1 cm in short axis, unchanged.

Lymph Nodes:
No significant mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. No aggressive osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Visualized portions are unremarkable.
impression:
1. Findings consistent with progression of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis with increased extent of fibrosis and traction bronchiectasis compared to prior study.
   Recommend follow-up CT in 6-12 months to assess for further progression.
2. Small bilateral pleural effusions.
3. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism or significant mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 65-year-old male presenting for evaluation of pulmonary fibrosis.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL of Iopamidol 76% IV solution. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 7/15/2022.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Extensive bilateral reticular opacities with honeycombing predominantly in the lower lobes, consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern. Patchy groundglass opacities superimposed on areas of fibrosis. Traction bronchiectasis and bronchiolectasis noted, more pronounced in the lower lobes. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
No evidence of pulmonary embolism. Main pulmonary artery measures within normal limits at 28 mm.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size normal. Mild pericardial effusion without tamponade. Thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications. Esophagus is non-distended but contains debris. Small hiatal hernia present.

Lymph Nodes:
No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy noted.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, including osteophytes and disc space narrowing. No suspicious osseous lesions. Mild bilateral gynecomastia.

Upper Abdomen:
Unremarkable except for a few hepatic cysts.

**
impression:
1. Bilateral pulmonary fibrosis with an imaging pattern consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia.
   Recommend follow-up CT scan in 6 months to monitor for progression.
2. Mild pericardial effusion without evidence of tamponade.
3. Small hiatal hernia.

Pulmonary Vasculature:

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:

report:
CLINICAL INFORMATION: 62-year-old male with a history of progressive shortness of breath and suspected pulmonary fibrosis.

PROCEDURE: Contrast-enhanced CT chest performed after IV administration of 100 mL Iohexol 350 mg/mL. Axial, coronal, and sagittal images reviewed. Thin sections obtained.

COMPARISON: Chest CT dated 5/10/2022.

FINDINGS:

Lungs and Pleura:
Extensive bilateral reticulation with honeycombing and traction bronchiectasis predominantly in the peripheral and basal lung regions, consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern. Scattered areas of groundglass opacity. Minimal bilateral pleural thickening. No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. No new or enlarging nodules.

Pulmonary Vasculature:
Main pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber with no filling defects to suggest pulmonary embolism. Mildly enlarged main pulmonary artery measuring up to 32 mm, possibly indicative of pulmonary hypertension.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Heart size is normal. No pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber with mild atherosclerotic calcifications. No aneurysm or dissection. Small hiatal hernia present.

Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

Skeleton and Chest Wall:
Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine. No suspicious osseous lesions.

Upper Abdomen:
Unremarkable visualized portions including the liver, spleen, pancreas, adrenals, and kidneys. Please see separately dictated abdominal CT for further details.

**
impression:
1. Findings consistent with usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) pattern indicative of pulmonary fibrosis.
2. Mildly enlarged main pulmonary artery suggestive of possible pulmonary hypertension.
3. No evidence of acute pulmonary embolism or new nodules.

Recommend follow-up high-resolution CT chest in 6-12 months to monitor progression of pulmonary fibrosis. Consider referral to pulmonology for management of interstitial lung disease and further evaluation for pulmonary hypertension.

report:

No filling defects in the main, lobar, segmental, or subsegmental pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism. Moderate dilation of the main pulmonary artery measuring up to 32 mm.

Mediastinum, Heart, and Great Vessels:
Normal heart size with mild left atrial enlargement. No pericardial effusion. Aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta show moderate atherosclerotic calcifications without aneurysm. Small hiatal hernia noted.

No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

impression:
